name: Project Zomboid
author: ops@refx.example
description: The Indie Stone's isometric zombie survival sim. SteamCMD-installed dedicated server with Workshop mod support and RCON administration.

installScript.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
cd /mnt/server
# Locate or bootstrap steamcmd (the parkervcp/steamcmd image does NOT ship it
# on PATH; eggs are expected to fetch it themselves).
if command -v steamcmd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  STEAMCMD=steamcmd
else
  echo "[steam] bootstrapping steamcmd"
  mkdir -p /mnt/server/steamcmd
  curl -sSL https://steamcdn-a.akamaihd.net/client/installer/steamcmd_linux.tar.gz | tar zxf - -C /mnt/server/steamcmd
  chmod +x /mnt/server/steamcmd/steamcmd.sh /mnt/server/steamcmd/linux32/steamcmd 2>/dev/null || true
  STEAMCMD=/mnt/server/steamcmd/steamcmd.sh
fi
"$STEAMCMD" +force_install_dir /mnt/server +login anonymous +app_update 380870 validate +quit
mkdir -p /mnt/server/Zomboid/Server
chmod +x start-server.sh || true
# Seed PZ's server preset "refx" (startup uses -servername refx). PublicName is the
# display name; -servername is just the config preset id (was wrongly the display name).
mkdir -p /mnt/server/Zomboid/Server
ZINI=/mnt/server/Zomboid/Server/refx.ini
if [ ! -f "$ZINI" ]; then
  cat > "$ZINI" <<EOF
PublicName=${SERVER_NAME}
MaxPlayers=${MAX_PLAYERS}
DefaultPort=${SERVER_PORT}
UDPPort=${UDP_PORT}
Mods=${MOD_IDS}
Public=true
Open=true
EOF
fi
# Apply the tier RAM to the JVM heap (PZ ignores SERVER_MEMORY otherwise → OOM on big plans).
for J in /mnt/server/ProjectZomboid64.json; do
  [ -f "$J" ] && sed -i -E "s/\"-Xmx[0-9]+[mg]\"/\"-Xmx${SERVER_MEMORY}m\"/" "$J" || true
done
echo 'Project Zomboid install complete.'

variables.description: Public server name and the name of the save/config set used.
Secondary UDP port used by the Steam direct-connect handshake.
Semicolon-separated list of Steam Workshop mod IDs to load.
Maximum Java heap size in megabytes for the server JVM.

longDescription: Persistent zombie-survival sandbox. Run a co-op Project Zomboid world with mods and custom sandbox settings.
